name: Chocolate Cake Florence
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. Butter and flour two 9-inch pans.
3. Sift together the cake flour, baking powder, and salt in a bowl.
4. In a large bowl, cream the butter until light and fluffy. Gradually add 1 1/2 cups of sugar, mixing well after each addition.
5. Beat in the egg yolks, then stir in the melted chocolate and vanilla extract.
6. Alternately add flour and milk to the mixture, mixing well after each addition.
7. In a separate bowl, beat the egg whites until stiff but not dry. Gradually fold into the batter.
8. Divide batter evenly between pans and bake for 35 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
9. Cool cakes completely.
10. Pour 2 tablespoons Tia Maria over each layer and frost with Florence icing. Dust the sides with chopped nuts.
Florence Icing:
4 tablespoons butter
2 cups sugar
1/2 cup milk
2 ounces bitter chocolate, melted
1. Melt butter in a saucepan and stir in sugar until well combined.
2. Gradually add milk to the mixture over medium heat, stirring constantly. Bring the mixture to a boil, then cook for 10 minutes or until the temperature reaches 236 degrees F (115 degrees C).
3. Remove from heat and stir in melted chocolate and Tia Maria.
4. Stir until thick enough to spread.
5. If icing hardens before frosting the cake, soften with a little hot water.
